{{Infobox concept
| name = Dreadgod
| introduced = Unsouled
}}

''See also: [[Dreadgod Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Dreadgods]] are massive, nearly immortal monsters characterized by an insatiable hunger to devour the world. They possess immense power, capable of consuming mountains or destroying entire valleys simply through their movement and presence. While they are powerful enough to challenge [[Monarchs]], the most advanced [[sacred artists]] must often unite to drive them off; notably, none have ever been successfully killed. Their spirits are composed of complex tapestries of [[madra]] woven through their physical forms, and they can utilize devastating techniques involving lightning, light, and blood-red energy. A Dreadgod's awakening can cause widespread destruction, such as earthquakes or the stripping of defenses from entire regions. They are often associated with various cults, such as [[Redmoon Hall]], which consist of followers who seek power by serving them or utilizing fragments of their [[essence]] known as [[Blood Shadows]]. The [[Dreadgods]] are linked to a mysterious [[labyrinth]] that is believed to be their birthplace or a site where they were studied. Within this location, there exists a progenitor known as [[Subject One]]—also referred to as the Father of Hunger or the fifth Dreadgod—who is considered the original from which the others were formed. Specific entities include the [[Bleeding Phoenix]], the [[Wandering Titan]], and the [[Empty Ghost]].}}

== Unsouled ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">1</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|A [[Dreadgod]] attack once claimed a quarter of the valley.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11">''Unsouled'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Skysworn ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|As an incomprehensibly vast creature that took to the sky, the Dreadgod represents a potential threat capable of killing everyone. This major entity causes bloodspawn to spread and utilizes servants to weaken individuals before its awakening. During its ascent, the creature emitted a bloody light that was too bright and served to strengthen the [[Blood Shadow]] parasite. Although its scream eventually quieted, the Dreadgod's massive form featured a sickle-like beak and could only have its body temporarily destroyed by [[Monarchs]].}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">12</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|A [[Dreadgod]] is a potential threat that could kill everyone.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c7">''Skysworn'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Dreadgod]] is a major threat that causes bloodspawn to spread.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c11">''Skysworn'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Dreadgod]] has servants that can weaken people before it wakes up.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The bloodspawn came from a [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c12">''Skysworn'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Dreadgod]]'s scream had quieted, but [[Stormrock]] was still rushing away.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c16">''Skysworn'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Dreadgod]] is described as being too bright.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Dreadgod]]'s light makes the [[Blood Shadow]] parasite stronger.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c17">''Skysworn'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Dreadgod]] emits a bloody light.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Yerin]] can cut bloodspawn off from the [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Dreadgod]] is an incomprehensibly vast creature that took to the sky.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Dreadgod]] has a sickle-like beak.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Monarchs]] can temporarily destroy the body of a [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c18">''Skysworn'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Ghostwater ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">1</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Dreadgod]]'s attack occurred in the town Enkai defended.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c1">''Ghostwater'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Underlord ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">2</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Dreadgod]]'s rampage broke the wall defending against the [[Trackless Sea]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c4">''Underlord'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|One [[Dreadgod]] has risen early.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c13">''Underlord'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|As a unique entity capable of being removed through [[Penance]], the Dreadgod exists as a looming threat that could potentially interrupt the tournament. This being possesses its own divine techniques and unique [[madra]], with expectations that it will rise within a few weeks. While the [[Abyssal Palace]] prepares for its arrival, the surrounding territory is being readied for a Dreadgod that possesses a home and intends to head toward [[Sacred Valley]] upon waking. As the entity moves toward [[Lindon]]'s home, [[Lindon]] begins gathering a team to confront it. Ultimately, the Dreadgod's spiritual pressure crashes over the Valley as the battle surrounding the creature becomes overwhelming.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">16</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A [[Dreadgod]] is the only being within a thousand miles capable of being [[Adama]]'s opponent.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c1">''Wintersteel'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A [[Dreadgod]] is a type of entity that can be removed using [[Penance]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c2">''Wintersteel'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A [[Dreadgod]] is mentioned as a potential interruption to the tournament.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|There is a [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3">''Wintersteel'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Mercy]] warns [[Pride]] not to poke any [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c5">''Wintersteel'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Dreadgod]] has unique [[madra]] and divine techniques.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c5" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Dreadgod]] is expected to rise in a few weeks.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c9">''Wintersteel'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Abyssal Palace]] is preparing for the coming of their [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c13">''Wintersteel'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Dreadgod]]'s will can lend strength to an [[Enforcer technique]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c14">''Wintersteel'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The territory was being prepared for the arrival of the [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c19">''Wintersteel'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Lindon]] is gathering a team to go to the [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c24">''Wintersteel'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Dreadgod]] has a home.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c24" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Dreadgod]] will head for [[Sacred Valley]] when it wakes.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c24" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Dreadgod]] is heading toward [[Lindon]]'s home.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c25">''Wintersteel'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The battle around the [[Dreadgod]] was becoming too much.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c28">''Wintersteel'', chapter 28</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Dreadgod]]'s spiritual pressure crashed over the Valley.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c28" />}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|As an insane, mountain-sized sacred beast, the Dreadgod moves slowly through the mountains toward Sky’s Edge and [[Lindon]]'s homeland. Its approach causes the earth to shake, affects the [[earth aura]], and presents an overwhelming threat capable of destroying cloudships with glancing blows. While clearing a path through the mountains to the east, the monster unleashes blood-red lightning and [[sword]]-[[madra]] slashes, its eyes appearing as swirling clouds of yellow. After being pierced by a lance of bright red [[madra]] and having its wrist impacted by the [[Hollow King]]'s [[Spear]], the Dreadgod eventually departs [[Sacred Valley]].}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">38</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]] is in the path of [[Lindon]]'s homeland.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c2">''Bloodline'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]] is currently feeding at Sky’s Edge.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A [[Dreadgod]] casts its shadow over the landscape.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c4">''Bloodline'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]]'s glancing blow was capable of destroying a cloudship.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]] is a being that [[Orthos]] might be in the path of.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c5">''Bloodline'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The approach of the [[Dreadgod]] affects the [[earth aura]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c6">''Bloodline'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Yerin]] wants to track her master's footsteps once a [[Dreadgod]] is no longer about to fall on them.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c6" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A [[Dreadgod]] attack is a threat to [[Lindon]]'s family.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c8">''Bloodline'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A [[Dreadgod]] is coming.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c9">''Bloodline'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A [[Dreadgod]] is an insane sacred beast as tall as a mountain.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c9" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A [[Dreadgod]] attack is an incoming danger.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c10">''Bloodline'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Dreadgods]] possess power that overwhelms all aspects of [[vital aura]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]] is an incoming threat whose footsteps can be felt in the earth.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The characters' immediate plans are to get away from the [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c10" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A [[Dreadgod]] is a monster.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c11">''Bloodline'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|There is concern regarding whether the clan can escape before the [[Dreadgod]] arrives.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c12">''Bloodline'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]] was moving slowly through the mountains.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c12" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]]'s approach causes ground shaking.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c13">''Bloodline'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A [[Dreadgod]] is something to be cautious of.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c13" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A [[Dreadgod]]'s heel can crush people.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c13" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Dreadgods]] are unable to retrieve their prize and will eventually forget its location.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c14">''Bloodline'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]] is clearing a way.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]] is a being that can move east and break through mountains.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c15">''Bloodline'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]] gives off an impression of overwhelming power.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Akura]] cloudships are moving away from the [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|There was a [[Dreadgod]] to the west and one to the east.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17">''Bloodline'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A [[Dreadgod]]'s attention can cause blood [[aura]] to flare in power.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]]'s head snapped up to focus on a new figure.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]]'s techniques are tinged blood-red.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]] can unleash lightning and [[sword]]-[[madra]] slashes.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Lindon]] considers fighting a [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18">''Bloodline'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]]'s eyes are swirling clouds of every shade of yellow.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19">''Bloodline'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]] has a wrist that was impacted by the [[Hollow King]]'s [[Spear]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A lance of bright red [[madra]] pierced the [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]]'s tail is falling on [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A [[Dreadgod]] is in [[Sacred Valley]] and is subject to physical laws there.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A [[Dreadgod]] is a powerful entity that [[Mercy's mother]]'s daughter faced down.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c21">''Bloodline'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]] is gone.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c21" />}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The rampage of a Dreadgod is an event that the [[Blackflame Empire]] would certainly notice, as even their footsteps are powerful enough to shake a tiny pond. Following the appearance of a second Dreadgod, the resulting destruction left [[Remnants]] within the [[Sacred Valley]]. Since the attack occurred, [[Elder Whisper]] has observed that no one has entered or exited the [[labyrinth]]. While a loss of power to the suppression field could potentially call the entity back, [[Mercy]]'s group did not emerge with a new Dreadgod.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">10</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Yerin]] mentions that her master did not leave a [[Dreadgod]] tucked away.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c3">''Reaper'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Blackflame Empire]] will not miss a [[Dreadgod]]'s rampage.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c4">''Reaper'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Fighting a [[Dreadgod]] causes exertion.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Ziel]] stated he was not going to fight the [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|A second [[Dreadgod]] appeared.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c5">''Reaper'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Dreadgod]]'s rampage left [[Remnants]] in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c6">''Reaper'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|A loss of power to the suppression field might call the [[Dreadgod]] back.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c8">''Reaper'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Elder Whisper]] has seen no one enter or leave the [[labyrinth]] since the attack of the [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c10">''Reaper'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Mercy]]'s group did not emerge with a new [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c25">''Reaper'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The footsteps of a [[Dreadgod]] can shake a tiny pond.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c26">''Reaper'', chapter 26</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Known as the [[Silent King]] and the tiger of the [[Silent Servants]] cult, this self-aware Dreadgod stalked forward toward a new lair in a dark cave located three hundred miles southwest of [[Lindon]]. Though it was the least-powerful of its kind, the entity sought slaves rather than victory and could cause disaster if underestimated. During a confrontation where its presence was felt near [[Yerin]], the Dreadgod aided in freeing a powerful spirit and attempted to wrestle against its opponents while assaulting [[Lindon]]'s mind. Although stolen power can temporarily hold back a Dreadgod, the entity was ultimately killed when [[Lindon]] shot it through the head with [[Blackflame]]. Following this battle, [[Lindon]] intended to use the creature's heart as a weapon, and he himself came to be considered the fourth Dreadgod.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">12</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgod]] is a potential entity that might trick [[Kerani]] into walking outside.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3">''Dreadgod'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgod]] is close enough to be a disaster.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] is not supposed to come near the [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgod]] wants slaves more than victory.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgod]] is stalking forward.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgod]] is referred to as the [[Silent King]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgod]]'s new lair is three hundred miles southwest of [[Lindon]]'s location.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgod]] sleeps in a dark cave.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Others must be brought to the level of fighting [[Dreadgods]] and [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgod]] mentioned is the least-powerful on its own.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c5">''Dreadgod'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Monarchs]] must fight against the [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c5" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Underestimating a [[Dreadgod]] can lead to death.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c10">''Dreadgod'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Redmoon]] gathers power to supplement the [[Dreadgod]]�s transportation.}}
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">12</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgod]]'s power was around [[Yerin]] but its presence was not felt.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c14">''Dreadgod'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A powerful spirit broke free with the aid of the [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15">''Dreadgod'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgod]]'s will was being wrestled against by some.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c16">''Dreadgod'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] dashed toward the [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A [[Dreadgod]]'s power can be held back by stolen power, though not forever.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18">''Dreadgod'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgod]] is the tiger of the [[Silent Servants]] cult.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgod]] assaulted [[Lindon]]'s mind.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgods]] are currently self-aware.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c19">''Dreadgod'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgod]] was shot through the head with [[Blackflame]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21">''Dreadgod'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] killed the [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] intends to carve out the heart of a [[Dreadgod]] to make it a weapon.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Wei Shi Lindon Arelius]] is considered the fourth [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c24">''Dreadgod'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Dreadgods]] are immense opponents that cannot be outlasted or exhausted, capable of generating illusions and altering the color of the sky through their [[aura]]. The [[Weeping Dragon]] is identified as one such entity, and it was noted that the [[Stormcallers]] also possess a Dreadgod. During a confrontation where the Archlords were in a Dreadgod's presence, the creature displayed a furious will and lashed out with lightning that fed on [[Lindon]]. While [[Lindon]] has previously borrowed the power of [[Dreadgods]] to defeat them, he eventually utilized the wills of two remaining [[Dreadgods]] to be hauled through space. These two entities died simultaneously following a roar, leaving behind the possibility that the inhabitants of a new world might actually be [[Dreadgods]] in human form.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">19</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The group has previously faced down a [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c3">''Waybound'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]]'s hand/will is compared to a [[Dreadgod]]'s.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Stormcallers]] have a [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c4">''Waybound'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Weeping Dragon]] is a [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c5">''Waybound'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Dreadgods]] can generate illusions.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c5" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]] is borrowing the power of the [[Dreadgods]] to defeat them.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6">''Waybound'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The clouds overhead were not signs of a coming [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c9">''Waybound'', chapter 9</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Dreadgods]] are opponents that cannot be exhausted or outlasted.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c10">''Waybound'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The others are stuck until they can face a [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c12">''Waybound'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The Archlords are in a [[Dreadgod]]'s presence.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c17">''Waybound'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|None of the characters are in a shape to fight a [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c18">''Waybound'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Lightning snaked out from the [[Dreadgod]] and fed on [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c19">''Waybound'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Dreadgods]] can affect the [[aura]] such that the sky changes colors when they are around.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c19" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Dreadgod]] possesses a furious will.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c19" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|There are two [[Dreadgods]] left.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c21">''Waybound'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Two [[Dreadgods]]' wills were used to haul [[Lindon]] through space via the horn.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c25">''Waybound'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Dreadgod]] roared.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c30">''Waybound'', chapter 30</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Two [[Dreadgods]] died at the same time.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c30" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The people in the new world might be human-form [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c36">''Waybound'', chapter 36</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">3</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Larian]] refers to [[Lindon]] as a [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c13">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|A [[Dreadgod]] passed.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c14">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Dreadgod]] cries out to switch back after the portal closes.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c14" />}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|“That depends,” Eithan said, “on whether or not there's a Dreadgod coming to kill us all.”<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c7" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|"Just cut them off from the mother," Yerin said, then winced. "...the Dreadgod."<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c17" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|“Try to be safe. Don’t poke any Dreadgods.”<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c5" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|"Once there�s no Dreadgod about to fall on us, then we can track my master�s footsteps."<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c6" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|“What we did learn is that the Dreadgods are unable to retrieve their prize, even without our intervention, and will soon forget its location and return to their random wandering.”<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c14" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:Unsouled]]
[[Category:Skysworn]]
[[Category:Ghostwater]]
[[Category:Underlord]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle concepts]]
[[Category:Lifeforms]]
